title: ICU respiratory deterioration documentation
source_review_basis: full authoritative documents inspected and archived; item-level retain/rewrite/remove/add comparisons recorded

assessment.text: Supports temperature, heart rate, respiratory rate, blood pressure, consciousness, oxygen saturation, skin signs, urine output, fever or rigors, and examination for the suspected infection source.
assessment.rationale: Supports respiratory rate, oxygen saturation and acute observations for ICU respiratory deterioration; no decision is generated.

source.title: Suspected sepsis in people aged 16 or over: recognition, assessment and early management
source.population: People aged 16 years and over with suspected sepsis, excluding people who are or have recently been pregnant.
source.setting: Community, ambulance, acute hospital, and emergency assessment settings.
source.exclusions: Exact for suspected sepsis recognition and assessment in non-pregnant people aged 16 years and over; partial for a broad fever or suspected-infection workflow and not directly validated for UAE pathways.

exact_location.heading: Recommendation 1.3 — history, observations, circulation, respiration, skin, urine output, and source examination

source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports temperature, heart rate, respiratory rate, blood pressure, consciousness, oxygen saturation, skin signs, urine output, fever or rigors, and examination for the suspected infection source.

assessment.text: Supports reassessment when symptoms or signs do not improve or worsen, clinician-led referral, microbiology where specified, and CRP or procalcitonin review in the qualified hospital treatment-failure context.
assessment.rationale: Supports clinician reassessment of pneumonia deterioration for ICU respiratory deterioration; no decision is generated.

source.title: Pneumonia: diagnosis and management
source.population: Children, young people, and adults with community- or hospital-acquired pneumonia, subject to recommendation-specific setting and age qualifiers.
source.setting: First contact, primary care, hospital, community treatment, reassessment, and post-discharge follow-up.
source.exclusions: Exact for selected pneumonia recovery, reassessment, observations, and post-inpatient chest-radiograph fields; international guidance requires UAE clinician review and the legacy workflow does not enforce every age or discharge qualifier.
source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports reassessment when symptoms or signs do not improve or worsen, clinician-led referral, microbiology where specified, and CRP or procalcitonin review in the qualified hospital treatment-failure context.

sources_used.title: Suspected sepsis in people aged 16 or over: recognition, assessment and early management
